title: Finite-mass P0 projection residuals are orthogonal to constants

source.citation: S. C. Brenner and L. R. Scott, The Mathematical Theory of Finite Element Methods, 3rd ed., Springer, 2008, Ch. 4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any finite mass vector with nonzero total mass, the residual from weighted P0 projection has zero mass-weighted inner product with every constant test value.
informal.latex: \sum_{i\in s} m_i (u_i-\bar u_m)c=0,\quad \bar u_m=(\sum_i m_i u_i)/(\sum_i m_i).
assumptions: s is a finite index set
m and u are real-valued functions on the index type
the total mass sum over s is nonzero
c is a real constant test value
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted residual is orthogonal to constant test functions in the mass inner product.

declarations.name: weightedP0Average
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Average {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) : Real := (∑ i ∈ s, m i * u i) / (∑ i ∈ s, m i)

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Residual {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - weightedP0Average s m u

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual_mass_zero
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Residual_mass_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : ∑ i ∈ s, m i * weightedP0Residual s m u i = 0

declarations.name: weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (c : Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : ∑ i ∈ s, m i * weightedP0Residual s m u i * c = 0

tasks.input: Translate mass-matrix P0 residual orthogonality to constants over an arbitrary finite index set into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (c : Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : ∑ i ∈ s, m i * weightedP0Residual s m u i * c = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by factoring the constant c out of the finite sum and using the mass-zero residual lemma.
tasks.target: Rewrite the sum as the residual mass sum times c, then use weightedP0Residual_mass_zero.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma that proves the mass-weighted P0 residual has zero total mass.
